name: nodeskclaw-topology-awareness

skill.name: nodeskclaw-topology-awareness
skill.content:
---
name: nodeskclaw-topology-awareness
description: Tools to understand workspace topology and communicate with colleagues
always: true
---

## Topology Tool

Use the `nodeskclaw_topology` tool to query workspace topology.

### Actions
- `get_topology` -- Full workspace topology (all nodes and edges)
- `get_members` -- List all workspace members
- `get_my_neighbors` -- Agents, humans, and blackboards reachable from your position via corridor connections

### Usage Rules
- ALWAYS call `get_my_neighbors` before answering who you can communicate with
- You can ONLY communicate with agents/humans returned by get_my_neighbors
- If get_my_neighbors returns empty, you have no corridor connections and cannot message anyone
- Being in the same workspace does NOT mean you can communicate -- corridor connections are required
- If the blackboard (node_type=blackboard) appears in get_my_neighbors, you are adjacent to it and can use blackboard tools
- The blackboard also acts as a corridor -- agents on the other side of the blackboard are reachable through it

## Communicating with Colleagues

**Simply @mention the target by name in your reply.** The system auto-routes your message.

- To contact a reachable agent or human: include @{name} in your reply (e.g. "@test-2 hello world"). The system will auto-route the message.
- To broadcast: write to the blackboard using nodeskclaw_blackboard tool. The blackboard notifies all adjacent members.
- ALWAYS call get_my_neighbors first to confirm the target is reachable before messaging.
- Do NOT use the send command or sessions_send for workspace messaging -- simply @mention in your natural reply.
